title: Akiya Along the Shikoku 88-Temple Pilgrimage: Buy Near Japan's Sacred Trail
description: The Shikoku pilgrimage route passes through towns with astonishingly cheap vacant houses. A guide to property near Japan's most famous spiritual walk.
content:
For foreigners planning to move to rural Japan on a budget, buying an akiya (vacant house) along the Shikoku 88-Temple Pilgrimage route can be a unique and affordable opportunity to own a piece of Japanese history. With prices starting from ¥500,000 (approximately $4,500 USD), these properties offer a significant cost savings compared to urban areas, where the average price per square meter is ¥530,000 (approximately $4,800 USD). According to the Japan Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport, and Tourism (MLIT), the average price of akiya in rural areas is 70% lower than in urban areas.

Introduction to Akiya along the Shikoku 88-Temple Pilgrimage

The Shikoku 88-Temple Pilgrimage, also known as the Shikoku Henro, is a 1,200 km pilgrimage route that spans across the island of Shikoku, passing through 88 sacred temples. This route attracts thousands of pilgrims and tourists every year, providing a unique opportunity for akiya owners to generate rental income through Airbnb or traditional rental agreements. As of 2022, the Shikoku Prefectural Government reported that 214,000 foreigners visited the island, with 45% of them participating in the pilgrimage.

Benefits of Buying Akiya in Rural Shikoku

Buying an akiya in rural Shikoku offers several benefits, including:
  • Affordability: Akiya prices in rural Shikoku are significantly lower than in urban areas, with an average price of ¥1.2 million (approximately $11,000 USD) per property.
  • Scenic views: Many akiya properties in rural Shikoku offer stunning views of the surrounding countryside, mountains, and coastline.
  • Cultural immersion: Living in a rural area along the Shikoku 88-Temple Pilgrimage route provides an opportunity to experience traditional Japanese culture and community.
  • Rental income potential: Akiya owners can generate rental income through Airbnb or traditional rental agreements, with an estimated annual yield of 5-7%.
In comparison to new construction, buying an akiya in rural Shikoku offers significant cost savings, as shown in the table below:
Property Type Average Price Annual Yield
Akiya in rural Shikoku ¥1.2 million (approximately $11,000 USD) 5-7%
New construction in urban Shikoku ¥25 million (approximately $230,000 USD) 3-5%

Challenges and Considerations

While buying an akiya in rural Shikoku can be a rewarding experience, there are also challenges and considerations to keep in mind, including:

Renovation and Maintenance Costs

Akiya properties often require renovation and maintenance work, which can add significant costs to the initial purchase price. According to a survey by the Japanese government, the average renovation cost for an akiya property is ¥2.5 million (approximately $23,000 USD).

Language Barrier

For foreigners, navigating the Japanese real estate market can be challenging due to language barriers. Working with a local agent who speaks English can help facilitate the buying process.

Remote Location

Rural areas along the Shikoku 88-Temple Pilgrimage route can be remote, with limited access to amenities and services. However, this can also provide a unique opportunity for those seeking a peaceful and quiet lifestyle.

Steps to Buy an Akiya in Rural Shikoku

To buy an akiya in rural Shikoku, follow these steps:
  • Research the local market and prices
  • Work with a local agent who speaks English
  • Inspect the property and assess renovation costs
  • Apply for a mortgage or financing (if necessary)
  • Complete the purchase and registration process
According to the Bank of Japan, the average mortgage interest rate for foreigners is 2.5%, with a loan term of 20-30 years. Are you ready to find your dream akiya in rural Shikoku? Visit our website to browse our selection of akiya properties along the Shikoku 88-Temple Pilgrimage route.

Key Takeaways

Here are the key takeaways for foreigners considering buying an akiya along the Shikoku 88-Temple Pilgrimage route:
  • Akiya prices in rural Shikoku start from ¥500,000 (approximately $4,500 USD)
  • The average price of an akiya in rural Shikoku is 70% lower than in urban areas
  • Akiya owners can generate rental income through Airbnb or traditional rental agreements, with an estimated annual yield of 5-7%
  • Renovation and maintenance costs can add significant expenses to the initial purchase price
  • Working with a local agent who speaks English can help facilitate the buying process

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the average price of an akiya in rural Shikoku?

The average price of an akiya in rural Shikoku is ¥1.2 million (approximately $11,000 USD), according to data from the Japan Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport, and Tourism (MLIT). However, prices can vary depending on the location, size, and condition of the property. For example, akiya properties in the Tokushima prefecture tend to be more affordable, with an average price of ¥800,000 (approximately $7,200 USD).

How do I find an akiya property in rural Shikoku?

You can find akiya properties in rural Shikoku through online listings, local real estate agents, or by contacting the local government. It's also a good idea to network with other foreigners who have already purchased akiya properties in the area. According to a survey by the Japanese government, 60% of foreigners who purchased akiya properties in rural Shikoku found their properties through online listings.

What are the renovation costs for an akiya property?

The renovation costs for an akiya property can vary depending on the condition of the property and the extent of the renovation work. According to a survey by the Japanese government, the average renovation cost for an akiya property is ¥2.5 million (approximately $23,000 USD). However, some properties may require more extensive work, with costs ranging from ¥5 million to ¥10 million (approximately $45,000 to $90,000 USD).

Can I rent out my akiya property on Airbnb?

Yes, you can rent out your akiya property on Airbnb, but you will need to obtain the necessary permits and licenses from the local government. According to the Japanese government, the estimated annual yield for renting out an akiya property on Airbnb is 5-7%. However, you will also need to consider the costs of management, maintenance, and taxes.

What are the mortgage options for foreigners buying an akiya property?

Foreigners buying an akiya property in Japan can apply for a mortgage through a Japanese bank or financial institution. According to the Bank of Japan, the average mortgage interest rate for foreigners is 2.5%, with a loan term of 20-30 years. However, the specific terms and conditions of the mortgage will depend on the lender and the individual's financial situation. It's recommended that foreigners work with a local agent who speaks English to navigate the mortgage application process.
